steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The $y$-intercept is the value of $y$ when $x = 0$. Substitute: $y = 250(4)^{2(0)} = 250(1) = 250$. Since $x$ counts six-month periods starting from the end of March 2001, $x = 0$ is that starting moment. The $y$-intercept means 250 subscribers at the end of March 2001.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of the first six-month period was 250.": that's $x = 1$, not $x = 0$. ✗
  • "The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of March 2001 was 250.": matches the $y$-intercept exactly. ✓
  • "The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of March 2002 was 1,000.": the $y$-intercept is a starting value, not a later value. ✗
  • "The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of March 2001 was 1,000.": uses the wrong subscriber count for the starting moment. ✗
That matches the correct answer - B is correct.
